title: cirsium americanum var. callilepis
intro: Cirsium americanum var. callilepis, also known as American Plume Thistle, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to North America. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of American Plume Thistle include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and relieve respiratory issues, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. American Plume Thistle can be prepared in various ways, including as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
